"Of all the advice my mother has instilled in me, wearing shoes inside the house took the longest to sink in. Some days, she’d threaten that I’d get sick if my feet touched the cold ground. Others, she’d warn of the agonizing pain I’d suffer if I were to step on a rogue shard of glass or catch my pinky toe on a corner. So many everyday dangers could befall me, I quickly lost count.

Turns out, she was right." https://trib.al/raZnD5R

For the first time since 1972, NASA is sending astronauts to the moon.

The mission, Artemis II, is scheduled to launch on Wednesday at 6:24 p.m. Eastern time. A giant rocket will lift off from Kennedy Space Center in Florida, and the crew of four astronauts — three Americans and one Canadian — will embark on a 10-day journey around the moon and back to Earth.

Reiterada em Kinshasa aposta no aprofundamento da integração económica

Angola reiterou, esta quarta-feira, em Kinshasa, a aposta no aprofundamento da integração económica e apelou aos empresários e investidores nacionais e da República Democrática do Congo (RDC) para transformarem o potencial comum em projectos concretos.

O Serviço de Investigação Criminal (SIC) em Malanje deteve, hoje, o director provincial do Instituto de Desenvolvimento Local (antigo Fundo de Apoio Social-FAS), Gomes António Golombole, por um alegado desvio de mais de mil milhões de kwanzas do Programa Kwenda.

Detido no Calumbo falso subcomissário da Polícia Nacional

O Serviço de Investigação Criminal (SIC) na província do Icolo e Bengo deteve, terça-feira, no município do Calumbo, um cidadão nacional, de 39 anos, que se fazia passar por subcomissário da Policia Nacional.

MIREMPET apresenta balanço da indústria diamantífera de 2025

O Ministério dos Recursos Minerais, Petróleo e Gás (MIREMPET) vai apresentar, no próximo dia 14 deste mês, o balanço da produção, comercialização e exportação de diamantes, referente ao ano de 2025 e as perspectivas para 2026.

The medical examiner in Buffalo has ruled that the death of a nearly blind man left alone by Border Patrol agents on a frigid night was a homicide, a finding that could lead to criminal charges.

Nurul Amin Shah Alam, a Rohingya refugee from Myanmar, died in February after the agents dropped him off outside a closed Tim Hortons doughnut shop. His death triggered outrage in Buffalo and around the U.S.

The racist gesture, made by a white Argentine tourist toward waiters at a Rio de Janeiro bar, was captured on video and quickly ricocheted across the internet.

In Brazil, the backlash was swift. The Argentine woman was arrested and charged with making a racist insult, a crime under Brazilian law. Now, the 29-year-old woman faces a possible prison sentence of two to five years and hefty fines, in a case that has kindled fierce debate in Brazil and Argentina.
